verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type-I single-pass transmembrane protein (classical_surface_receptor architecture) with an ectodomain that binds JUNO on the egg surface. However, its surface display is restricted to a narrow, germline/gamete-specific context: in non-acrosome-reacted sperm it resides on the inner acrosomal membrane, and it only translocates to the outer sperm plasma membrane (equatorial segment) upon acrosome reaction during fertilization. Expression is entirely sperm-specific; IZUMO1 is not displayed on somatic cell surfaces. Because surface exposure is confined to a narrow germline/reproductive lineage and requires the acrosome reaction trigger, this maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes.
